ayahEnglish: A supplicant asked for a punishment bound to happen
A supplicant asked for a punishment bound to happen

To the disbelievers; of it there is no preventer.

targetTranslation: a repeller/one who repels (subject of لَيْسَ)
ayahEnglish: To the disbelievers; of it there is no preventer.
[It is] from Allāh, owner of the ways of ascent.
[It is] from Allāh, owner of the ways of ascent.

ayahArabic: تَعْرُجُ ٱلْمَلٰٓئِكَةُ وَٱلرُّوحُ إِلَيْهِ فِى يَوْمٍ كَانَ مِقْدَارُهُۥ خَمْسِينَ أَلْفَ سَنَةٍ
ayahEnglish: The angels and the Spirit [i.e., Gabriel] will ascend to Him during a Day the extent of which is fifty thousand years.

ayahArabic: تَعْرُجُ ٱلْمَلٰٓئِكَةُ وَٱلرُّوحُ إِلَيْهِ فِى يَوْمٍ كَانَ مِقْدَارُهُۥ خَمْسِينَ أَلْفَ سَنَةٍ
ayahEnglish: The angels and the Spirit [i.e., Gabriel] will ascend to Him during a Day the extent of which is fifty thousand years.

targetTranslation: patience (as the object of فَٱصْبِرْ - have patience)

ayahEnglish: But We see it [as] near.
On the Day the sky will be like murky oil,
On the Day the sky will be like murky oil,
And no friend will ask [anything of] a friend,

targetTranslation: they are made to see them (the sinners will see their loved ones but can't help them)
ayahArabic: يُبَصَّرُونَهُمْ يَوَدُّ ٱلْمُجْرِمُ لَوْ يَفْتَدِى مِنْ عَذَابِ يَوْمِئِذٍۭ بِبَنِيهِ
ayahEnglish: They will be shown each other. The criminal will wish that he could be ransomed from the punishment of that Day by his children.

ayahArabic: يُبَصَّرُونَهُمْ يَوَدُّ ٱلْمُجْرِمُ لَوْ يَفْتَدِى مِنْ عَذَابِ يَوْمِئِذٍۭ بِبَنِيهِ
ayahEnglish: They will be shown each other. The criminal will wish that he could be ransomed from the punishment of that Day by his children.

targetTranslation: which (referring to فَصِيلَتِهِ - his closest relatives)
ayahEnglish: And his nearest kindred who shelter him.

ayahArabic: وَمَن فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ جَمِيعًا ثُمَّ يُنجِيهِ
ayahEnglish: And whoever is on earth entirely [so] then it could save him.

ayahArabic: وَمَن فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ جَمِيعًا ثُمَّ يُنجِيهِ
ayahEnglish: And whoever is on earth entirely [so] then it could save him.

targetTranslation: Blazing Fire (as a proper name for a level of Hell)

ayahEnglish: And those who are fearful of the punishment of their Lord -

ayahEnglish: And those who are fearful of the punishment of their Lord -
Indeed, the punishment of their Lord is not that from which one is safe -

ayahArabic: إِلَّا عَلَىٰٓ أَزْوٰجِهِمْ أَوْ مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمٰنُهُمْ فَإِنَّهُمْ غَيْرُ مَلُومِينَ
ayahEnglish: Except from their wives or those their right hands possess, for indeed, they are not to be blamed -

ayahArabic: فَمَنِ ٱبْتَغَىٰ وَرَآءَ ذٰلِكَ فَأُو۟لٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْعَادُونَ
ayahEnglish: But whoever seeks beyond that, then they are the transgressors -

ayahEnglish: So what is [the matter] with those who disbelieve, hastening [from] before you, [O Muḥammad],
[To sit] on [your] right and [your] left in separate groups?

targetTranslation: does he eagerly want? (referring to entering Paradise)
ayahArabic: أَيَطْمَعُ كُلُّ ٱمْرِئٍ مِّنْهُمْ أَن يُدْخَلَ جَنَّةَ نَعِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: Does every person among them aspire to enter a garden of pleasure?

No! Indeed, We have created them from that which they know.
No! Indeed, We have created them from that which they know.

targetTranslation: I swear (negated by لَا before it)
ayahArabic: فَلَآ أُقْسِمُ بِرَبِّ ٱلْمَشٰرِقِ وَٱلْمَغٰرِبِ إِنَّا لَقٰدِرُونَ
ayahEnglish: So I swear by the Lord of [all] risings and settings that indeed We are able

ayahArabic: عَلَىٰٓ أَن نُّبَدِّلَ خَيْرًا مِّنْهُمْ وَمَا نَحْنُ بِمَسْبُوقِينَ
ayahEnglish: To replace them with better than them; and We are not to be outdone.

targetTranslation: better ones (object of نُّبَدِّلَ - we will replace)
ayahArabic: عَلَىٰٓ أَن نُّبَدِّلَ خَيْرًا مِّنْهُمْ وَمَا نَحْنُ بِمَسْبُوقِينَ
ayahEnglish: To replace them with better than them; and We are not to be outdone.

targetTranslation: we (as the subject of نُّبَدِّلَ - we replace)
ayahArabic: عَلَىٰٓ أَن نُّبَدِّلَ خَيْرًا مِّنْهُمْ وَمَا نَحْنُ بِمَسْبُوقِينَ
ayahEnglish: To replace them with better than them; and We are not to be outdone.

targetTranslation: they plunge into (connects to وَيَلْعَبُوا۟ - and they play)
ayahArabic: فَذَرْهُمْ يَخُوضُوا۟ وَيَلْعَبُوا۟ حَتَّىٰ يُلٰقُوا۟ يَوْمَهُمُ ٱلَّذِى يُوعَدُونَ
ayahEnglish: So leave them to converse vainly and amuse themselves until they meet their Day which they are promised -

ayahArabic: فَذَرْهُمْ يَخُوضُوا۟ وَيَلْعَبُوا۟ حَتَّىٰ يُلٰقُوا۟ يَوْمَهُمُ ٱلَّذِى يُوعَدُونَ
ayahEnglish: So leave them to converse vainly and amuse themselves until they meet their Day which they are promised -

ayahEnglish: So leave them to converse vainly and amuse themselves until they meet their Day which they are promised -

targetTranslation: who/which/that (connects to يُوعَدُونَ - they are promised)

ayahEnglish: So leave them to converse vainly and amuse themselves until they meet their Day which they are promised -

ayahArabic: فَذَرْهُمْ يَخُوضُوا۟ وَيَلْعَبُوا۟ حَتَّىٰ يُلٰقُوا۟ يَوْمَهُمُ ٱلَّذِى يُوعَدُونَ
ayahEnglish: So leave them to converse vainly and amuse themselves until they meet their Day which they are promised -

targetTranslation: on the Day (when يَخْرُجُونَ they will emerge)
ayahArabic: يَوْمَ يَخْرُجُونَ مِنَ ٱلْأَجْدَاثِ سِرَاعًا كَأَنَّهُمْ إِلَىٰ نُصُبٍ يُوفِضُونَ
ayahEnglish: The Day they will emerge from the graves rapidly as if they were, toward an erected idol, hastening.

targetTranslation: they go out/exit (referring to the disbelievers emerging from their graves on the Day of Judgment)
ayahArabic: يَوْمَ يَخْرُجُونَ مِنَ ٱلْأَجْدَاثِ سِرَاعًا كَأَنَّهُمْ إِلَىٰ نُصُبٍ يُوفِضُونَ
ayahEnglish: The Day they will emerge from the graves rapidly as if they were, toward an erected idol, hastening.

targetTranslation: quickly (describing how they emerge from graves)

ayahEnglish: The Day they will emerge from the graves rapidly as if they were, toward an erected idol, hastening.

ayahArabic: يَوْمَ يَخْرُجُونَ مِنَ ٱلْأَجْدَاثِ سِرَاعًا كَأَنَّهُمْ إِلَىٰ نُصُبٍ يُوفِضُونَ
ayahEnglish: The Day they will emerge from the graves rapidly as if they were, toward an erected idol, hastening.

ayahArabic: يَوْمَ يَخْرُجُونَ مِنَ ٱلْأَجْدَاثِ سِرَاعًا كَأَنَّهُمْ إِلَىٰ نُصُبٍ يُوفِضُونَ
ayahEnglish: The Day they will emerge from the graves rapidly as if they were, toward an erected idol, hastening.

ayahArabic: خٰشِعَةً أَبْصٰرُهُمْ تَرْهَقُهُمْ ذِلَّةٌ ذٰلِكَ ٱلْيَوْمُ ٱلَّذِى كَانُوا۟ يُوعَدُونَ
ayahEnglish: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. That is the Day which they had been promised.

ayahArabic: خٰشِعَةً أَبْصٰرُهُمْ تَرْهَقُهُمْ ذِلَّةٌ ذٰلِكَ ٱلْيَوْمُ ٱلَّذِى كَانُوا۟ يُوعَدُونَ
ayahEnglish: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. That is the Day which they had been promised.

targetTranslation: she/it overwhelms them (referring to the disbelievers)
ayahArabic: خٰشِعَةً أَبْصٰرُهُمْ تَرْهَقُهُمْ ذِلَّةٌ ذٰلِكَ ٱلْيَوْمُ ٱلَّذِى كَانُوا۟ يُوعَدُونَ
ayahEnglish: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. That is the Day which they had been promised.

ayahArabic: خٰشِعَةً أَبْصٰرُهُمْ تَرْهَقُهُمْ ذِلَّةٌ ذٰلِكَ ٱلْيَوْمُ ٱلَّذِى كَانُوا۟ يُوعَدُونَ
ayahEnglish: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. That is the Day which they had been promised.

targetTranslation: that (pointing to something far) (refers to ٱلْيَوْمُ - the Day)
ayahArabic: خٰشِعَةً أَبْصٰرُهُمْ تَرْهَقُهُمْ ذِلَّةٌ ذٰلِكَ ٱلْيَوْمُ ٱلَّذِى كَانُوا۟ يُوعَدُونَ
ayahEnglish: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. That is the Day which they had been promised.

targetTranslation: the one who/which/that (refers to يَوْمُ - the Day)
ayahArabic: خٰشِعَةً أَبْصٰرُهُمْ تَرْهَقُهُمْ ذِلَّةٌ ذٰلِكَ ٱلْيَوْمُ ٱلَّذِى كَانُوا۟ يُوعَدُونَ
ayahEnglish: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. That is the Day which they had been promised.

ayahArabic: خٰشِعَةً أَبْصٰرُهُمْ تَرْهَقُهُمْ ذِلَّةٌ ذٰلِكَ ٱلْيَوْمُ ٱلَّذِى كَانُوا۟ يُوعَدُونَ
ayahEnglish: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. That is the Day which they had been promised.

targetTranslation: they are being promised/warned (referring to the disbelievers mentioned in أَبْصٰرُهُمْ)
ayahArabic: خٰشِعَةً أَبْصٰرُهُمْ تَرْهَقُهُمْ ذِلَّةٌ ذٰلِكَ ٱلْيَوْمُ ٱلَّذِى كَانُوا۟ يُوعَدُونَ
ayahEnglish: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. That is the Day which they had been promised.
